instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-positive` that evaluates its argument once, signals an error if the value is not a positive number, and returns the value otherwise.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (- X 1)))
            (UNLESS (AND (NUMBERP #:G1) (PLUSP #:G1))
              (ERROR "Expected positive number, got ~S" #:G1))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (- X 1)))
            (UNLESS (AND (NUMBERP #:G112) (PLUSP #:G112))
              (ERROR "Expected positive number, got ~S" #:G112))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
